title: Lithocarpus tephrocarpus
taxonomy: Lithocarpus tephrocarpus belongs to the family Fagaceae and is a species of tree. It is classified as a genus within the tribe Quereceae. The species is native to Southeast Asia and is commonly found in the tropical rainforests of Indonesia, Malaysia, and the Philippines. Lithocarpus tephrocarpus is also known as the "tropical Lithocarpus" and is closely related to other species within the same genus. Its classification as a Fagaceae family member makes it part of the beech family.
uses: Lithocarpus tephrocarpus, commonly known as the Taiwan corkbark or Taiwan red bark, has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. Its bark contains various bioactive compounds with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. Traditional healers in Taiwan have used the plant for treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential health benefits. However, preliminary studies suggest that Lithocarpus tephrocarpus may have promise as a natural remedy for certain health issues.
benefits: Lithocarpus tephrocarpus, also known as the Burmese walnut, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. Its extracts have been studied for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help protect against cell damage and reduce inflammation in the body. Additionally, Lithocarpus tephrocarpus has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. The plant's extracts have been studied for their antioxidant and free radical scavenging activities, suggesting its potential in protecting against oxidative stress and cell damage. These compounds have been found to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. Flavonoids, in particular, have been shown to have cardio-protective and neuroprotective effects. The plant's phenolic acids have also been found to exhibit anti-cancer properties. intro: Lithocarpus tephrocarpus, also known as the Burmese tanbark oak, is a species of plant in the beech family (Fagaceae) native to Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, offering various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and sore throat. The bioactive constituents of Lithocarpus tephrocarpus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often made into herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including respiratory and gastrointestinal issues.
